resources.string: DPTV Cursor - A mouse cursor for dumbphones and TVs
Control mouse cursor on your Android dumphone / TV

DPTV Cursor uses AccessibilityService to move an on-screen cursor, perform taps, and scroll for you across apps. Before the app opens Accessibility settings, you must explicitly choose whether to continue.

DPTV Cursor uses AccessibilityService so you can control an on-screen cursor across apps, perform taps, and scroll using your device keys.

When enabled, Android allows the service to observe the active screen and interact with UI elements in order to move the cursor and perform those actions.

The service is only used for the cursor features you request inside the app. It is not required to use your device normally.

Select Continue to open Android Accessibility settings and enable DPTV Cursor, or select Not now to cancel.

• Accessibility permission is required so the cursor can move, tap, and scroll across apps.

• Overlay permission is required so the cursor can show on top of apps.

• The app now shows a separate accessibility disclosure before Android settings open. Choose Continue only if you want to enable that service.

• Both services must stay active or the cursor will not appear or respond.

Press and hold any key for about 3 seconds, then release it to set it as your Shortcut Key.

The Shortcut Key lets you quickly enable or disable the mouse service, and also switch between Scroll mode and D-Pad mode.

If no popup appears after pressing a key, it means that key is not supported.
